Description: Identifies the study as a fairness audit of a prediction model
Structured abstract with background, methods, fairness findings, conclusions
Fairness audit framed in context of algorithmic disparities in ICU care
MIMIC-IV v2.2 (development) + eICU v2.0 (external validation)
Eligibility criteria identical across datasets
In-hospital mortality, operationalized consistently
43 first-24h features (20 vitals, 22 labs, 2 clinical); gcs_total and lab_troponin excluded due to 100% missingness — retained as binary missingness indicators per item 8a
No formal sample size calculation; >50K train, >100K validation
Binary missingness indicators; median imputation from training set
Novel: demographic-structured missingness as bias pathway
LR, RF, XGBoost; 5-fold CV; composite calibration criterion
ECE, slope, intercept; subgroup-specific; four recalibration strategies
AUROC, AUPRC, Brier, sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, F1
eICU: no retraining; preprocessing from MIMIC-IV only
Five formal fairness criteria: EO, EqOpp, DP, PP, CalFair
Race/ethnicity, sex, insurance, age group, ICU type
Four strategies compared; Pareto frontier analysis
SHAP (global + race-stratified + missingness SHAP)
Group-stratified DCA across race and insurance subgroups
TRIPOD+AI (Collins et al., BMJ 2024)
Full pipeline available at GitHub
